headings: The Immutability Constraint — and Why It Forces a Pattern
Sidecar .meta.md — When the Row Isn't Enough
Naming Convention — Soft, Scanned, Not Enforced
Bootstrap behavior on bcos init in a new repo
documents.first_paragraph: How docs/_collections/ works in CLEAR Context OS — what it holds, why files there are immutable, how the manifest pattern makes binary cargo first-class evidence, how its vocabularies grow with the business, and how it stays distinct from _wiki/, _inbox/, and active data points.

documents.name: component-standards
documents.first_paragraph: How to add a new skill, agent, or hook to the CLEAR Context OS ecosystem.

documents.name: content-routing
headings: Path 6 — Outbound from active zone via lifecycle classifier
documents.first_paragraph: How new content enters, flows through, and settles into the right location in CLEAR Context OS. This document covers the mechanics -- for the business rationale behind folder zones and ownership, see docs/_bcos-framework/architecture/system-design.md.

documents.name: context-routing
headings: Source-of-Truth Ranking — How Conflict Resolution Works
How Routing Composes With the Rest of the Architecture
documents.first_paragraph: How CLEAR Context OS turns "I'm about to do task T" into a curated, freshness-flagged, source-of-truth-aware bundle of context. Routing is a derived layer on top of context-zones.md (zones), metadata-system.md (per-doc metadata), and the typed-edge graph in context-index.json (builds-on, references, depends-on, provides).

documents.name: context-zones
headings: How Zones Compose with Other Architecture
documents.first_paragraph: How CLEAR Context OS classifies every file under docs/ into a **zone** — a typed region with its own trust level, freshness model, and source-of-truth role. The zone is what tells the framework whether a doc is canonical truth, derived explanation, frozen evidence, future intent, or historical record.

documents.name: Lifecycle Routing Architecture
documents.first_paragraph: How CLEAR Context OS routes active documents to their permanent homes — _archive/, _wiki/, or _collections/ — using a declarative rules table and a classifier that composes frontmatter triggers, body-signal markers, and reality cross-checks.

documents.name: maintenance-lifecycle
headings: Maintenance Lifecycle -- How BCOS Stays Healthy
documents.first_paragraph: Context dies from neglect, drift, accumulation, ownership vacuums, and change blindness. The maintenance lifecycle is the defense system: layered checks at increasing time horizons that catch problems before they compound.

documents.name: metadata-system
documents.first_paragraph: Every managed document in CLEAR Context OS carries YAML frontmatter metadata. This metadata enables automated health checks (hooks and scripts), discovery (Document Index generation), and enforcement (context-audit). This is the single reference for all validation rules.

documents.name: system-design
headings: Tier 1 -- Foundation (always active or frequently invoked)
What Stays in the Main Window
documents.first_paragraph: CLEAR Context OS (BCOS) is a framework that turns a Git repository into a living knowledge base managed by Claude Code. It layers a skill/agent/hook system on top of structured Markdown documents so that organizational knowledge stays accurate, bounded, and discoverable across sessions. The architecture separates *what the business knows* (docs/) from *how that knowledge is maintained* (.claude/)…

documents.name: typed-events
documents.first_paragraph: How the dispatcher's daily digest emits **structured events** alongside the human-readable prose, so the dashboard can render one-click cards without re-parsing strings, and so resolutions.jsonl (see maintenance-lifecycle.md) can carry typed finding_type + finding_attrs from day 1.

documents.name: Wiki Headless Scripts

meta.name: Wiki Headless Scripts
meta.description: Architecture, contract, and feedback loop for the four wiki maintenance scripts that bypass chat for cron + dashboard execution.

documents.first_paragraph: The wiki zone has four maintenance jobs (stale-propagation, source-refresh, graveyard, coverage-audit). Each one has TWO execution paths: a **chat-driven** path that reads the matching references/job-wiki-*.md and executes step-by-step, and a **headless** path that runs a Python script in subprocess. This file pins the headless contract.

Designed to be AI-friendly today and graph/RAG-ready tomorrow.

strictly_avoids: Business truth, strategic decisions, and source content owned by canonical data points.

documents.name: document-index
documents.first_paragraph: This file has two sections:

skills.description: Wiki zone manager for BCOS. Ingests URLs / local docs / inbox captures into docs/_wiki/ as schema-validated pages with banner citations, three structural shapes for source-summary, and two-tier refresh. Schema-driven, CLEAR-governed, derived-artifact. Invoke with /wiki.
Extracts structured context from conversation exports (Slack, meeting transcripts, chat logs) into _inbox for processing.

Cross-zone retrieval and task-driven bundle resolution for BCOS. /context search ranks docs across every zone in one shared schema; /context bundle (P5) returns a curated, freshness-flagged, source-of-truth-aware context bundle for a declared task. Mechanical-first; LLM is opt-in via --semantic / --resolve-conflicts / --verify-coverage flags only. Invoke with /context.
Bootstrap skill - enforces proactive skill discovery and invocation. Automatically check for applicable skills before any action. Always active.
Strategic reflection skill. Step back from daily work to think about the bigger picture of your context architecture, identify gaps, and envision improvements.
Agent ecosystem expert for creating, maintaining, and auditing agents and skills. Prevents ecosystem drift and overlap.

Manage the BCOS self-learning ladder: list learned rules, forget a rule, regenerate learned-rules.json from resolutions.jsonl. Mechanical-only — reads/writes derived artifacts; the actual learning happens via record_resolution.py at click time. Invoke with /learning.
Periodic maintenance of institutional knowledge. Analyzes lessons for staleness, overlaps, and gaps. Keeps lessons.json healthy.

jobs.about: Monthly full architecture + ecosystem pass. Quarterly is fine once context is mature — just change 'schedule' to a cron like '0 0 1 */3 *' or ask Claude to do it.
Deep CLEAR audit + inbox processing. Weekly by default; bump to twice-weekly if the inbox grows faster than you can triage.
Friday weekly safety brake on the self-learning ladder. Surfaces amber cards when a learned rule reversal rate crosses 5% in 7 days. Recommend-only at v0.1.
Deeper mid-week daydream — gaps, structural change. Skip in new projects where context is still forming.
Strategic reflection + lessons capture. Once a week is usually enough.
Rebuild document index + scan for frontmatter, metadata, and cross-reference issues. Cheap — always run daily.

Friday weekly classifier of active-zone docs against lifecycle-routing.yml. Surface-only for the first 2 weeks (default); flip lifecycle-routing.yml > global.surface_only=false after 0 false-positives confirmed to enable auto-routing.

Daily mechanical scan: wiki captures whose numeric facts diverge from canonical docs whose last-updated is > 180 days. Emits wiki-canonical-drift-suggestion findings; never edits canonical docs. Schema 1.2 Class D — see job-wiki-canonical-drift.md.
Quarterly cross-zone coverage scan. Surfaces active data points without wiki explainers and wiki clusters not present in document-index as INFO.
Monthly archive-candidate scan for stale/orphaned/expired wiki pages. Suggests archive actions; only whitelisted post-mortem expiry is auto-fixable.
Two-tier source-summary refresh: HEAD-only quick checks at stale_threshold_days/4, full refresh-must-rediscover at stale_threshold_days.
Scan wiki pages whose builds-on sources changed after last-reviewed. Cheap metadata-only check; emits action items for human review.

plans.task_description: Implement 8 MemPalace-inspired features for automatic session capture, session continuity, knowledge infrastructure, and archive intelligence
Move framework docs to docs/_bcos-framework/, commit pending session fixes, update all 108 references across 33 files

BCOS Dashboard cockpit-and-drawer rebuild, Steps 4-7. Move the four standalone panels still on the main page into either a click-driven side drawer (jobs panel) or a /settings route (run history, file health, snapshot canary). Then polish empty/loading/error states and accessibility/responsive/dark-mode. Each step ships independently as its own commit.

Fix systemic ecosystem state.json drift across BCOS-enabled repos by replacing additive-merge with disk-scan regeneration, wiring into install/daily/monthly checkpoints, and adding ecosystem-state-refresh to the auto-fix whitelist.

Upgrade Context Galaxy from force-graph-with-halos to interactive space scene with real orbits and document navigation. Includes 3 fixes (commit hygiene, library decision, dynamic re-layout) and 5 visual/interaction upgrades (starfield, orbits, wiki/collections styling, doc nav, polish).

Plan and implement the next Context Galaxy UX/action foundation: smoother focus, polished visual states, preview-first drawer, safe mechanical actions, LLM-assisted proposal actions, context-index integration hooks, and stronger browser/API verification.

Wiki-zone enhancements derived from llm-wiki review: authority semantics (1), temporal/supersession + 4-class contradiction triage (2), dispatcher-routed conflict findings (4), dashboard forms for ingest user actions (3, gated), and explicit deferral decision for synthesis page-type (5). Schema migration 1.1 -> 1.2; 4 new typed-event finding IDs; 2 new auto-fix IDs; 1 new dispatcher job; 3 new headless dashboard scripts; revised wake-up context surfacing authority hierarchy.

Implement Autonomy + UX + Self-Learning surface for the schedule-dispatcher / digest / dashboard / learning ecosystem. Eight phases (P8 conditional) plus P0 foundation and P9 fixed-end. Migrate digest action items from prose to typed events; introduce dashboard cards with one-click headless actions; record every fix to resolutions.jsonl with full schema from day 1; build a self-learning ladder (preselect -> auto-apply with undo -> silent) with an auto-fix auditor as the safety brake; surface everything in /settings/learning + /settings/auditor cockpit panels. Mechanical-first throughout; LLM only for P8 semantic-drift check (gated behind --semantic flag).

Build lifecycle-sweep system as a BCOS framework feature shipped in business-context-os-dev. (1) Optional `lifecycle:` frontmatter field with declared exit-trigger schema; (2) Declarative routing config `.claude/quality/lifecycle-routing.yml` covering routes to _archive/, _wiki/, _collections/, _planned/; (3) `lifecycle_sweep.py` job that reads each file's frontmatter + body + cross-checks reality, extends the live typed-event enum + headless-actions registry from autonomy-ux-self-learning, calls record_resolution.py for each routing decision so promote_resolutions.py picks up patterns into the self-learning ladder automatically. Generalized for any BCOS user. theo-portfolio is the first acceptance-test install via update.py.

Build the missing layers of BCOS's context base: cross-zone retrieval surface, sub-agent isolation for big-body work, wiki-zone stub repair, and task-driven cross-zone routing. Wiki is one zone among several. Plan must work for empty and populated repos. Mechanical-first principle (D-10): LLM is opt-in escalation only, never auto-fires. Foundation reuses the canonical context_index.py / context-index.json shipped on 2026-05-04 (commit 871ff4d).
